title: Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple
tagline: Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu and located in Thiruvanvandoor, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India.
overview.description: The Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu and located in Thiruvanvandoor, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Paambanaiappan. The nearest railway station to the temple is located in Chengannur, while the nearest airport is Trivandrum International Airport. It is one of the five ancient shrines i

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mazhukeer
topPlaces.description: Mazhukeer is a village that lies in the Alappuzha district of Kerala in India. The village begins at Arattupuzha Bridge, which connects Pathanamthitta district and Allapuzha district and lies up to a church near Kallissery junction.

topPlaces.name: Karingattukavu Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: The Karingattukavu Devi Temple is located at Prayar village, Chengannur taluk, Kerala, in India on the bank of the river Pampa. It is a Durga and Bhadrakali temple.

topPlaces.name: Adichikkavu Sree Durga Devi Kshetram
topPlaces.description: Adichikkavu Sree Durga Devi Kshetram, also known as the Adichikavu (Adichicavu) Devi Temple (Malayalam: അടിചിക്കാവ് ശ്രീ ദുര്‍ഗ ദേവി ക്ഷേത്രം) is one of the oldest temple in Pandanad village, Alapuzha. The temple is located at Pandanad in Chengannur taluk of Alappuzha district in the south Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Mundankavu
topPlaces.description: Mundankavu is a village situated 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) from Chengannur town centre in Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It lies on the banks of the river Pampa.

topPlaces.name: Kadavil Malika
topPlaces.description: Kadavil Malika is a historic place in India, associated with the origin of the Maramon Convention. It is a house in Kallissery on the banks of River Pamba and was built by Unnittan Kathanar (1767–1852) and his son Abraham Kathanar (1822–1884) also known as Kadavil Achen, in the early 19th century.

topPlaces.name: Thrichittatt Maha Vishnu Temple
topPlaces.description: The Thrichittatt Mahavishnu Temple (also called Thiruchenkundrur and Imayavarappan temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Chengannur, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, by Nammalvar, one of the Sri Vaishnava saint-poets of the 8th century called the Alvars.

topPlaces.name: Kallisseri Azhakiyakavu Devi Temple, Chengannur
topPlaces.description: Azhakiyakavu Devi Temple is situated at Kallisseri in Chengannur taluk of Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali and is one of the 30 temples in Central Travancore area where the ancient Dravidian ritualistic art form of Padayani is performed annually.

topPlaces.name: Manimala River
topPlaces.description: Manimala River, or Manimalayar, is a 92 km long river which flows through South Kerala. The river used to be wrongly considered as a tributary of Pamba River before satellite maps became popular, but this was proved incorrect.

items.note: Mazhukeer is a village that lies in the Alappuzha district of Kerala in India. The village begins at Arattupuzha Bridge, which connects Pathanamthitta district and Allapuzha district and lies up to a church near Kallissery junction.
The Karingattukavu Devi Temple is located at Prayar village, Chengannur taluk, Kerala, in India on the bank of the river Pampa. It is a Durga and Bhadrakali temple.

Adichikkavu Sree Durga Devi Kshetram, also known as the Adichikavu (Adichicavu) Devi Temple (Malayalam: അടിചിക്കാവ് ശ്രീ ദുര്‍ഗ ദേവി ക്ഷേത്രം) is one of the oldest temple in Pandanad village, Alapuzha. The temple is located at Pandanad in Chengannur taluk of Alappuzha district in the south Indian state of Kerala.
Mundankavu is a village situated 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) from Chengannur town centre in Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It lies on the banks of the river Pampa.

Kadavil Malika is a historic place in India, associated with the origin of the Maramon Convention. It is a house in Kallissery on the banks of River Pamba and was built by Unnittan Kathanar (1767–1852) and his son Abraham Kathanar (1822–1884) also known as Kadavil Achen, in the early 19th century.

The Thrichittatt Mahavishnu Temple (also called Thiruchenkundrur and Imayavarappan temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu, located in Chengannur, Alappuzha District, Kerala, South India. Constructed in the Kerala style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, by Nammalvar, one of the Sri Vaishnava saint-poets of the 8th century called the Alvars.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Kallisseri Azhakiyakavu Devi Temple, Chengannur & around

items.note: Azhakiyakavu Devi Temple is situated at Kallisseri in Chengannur taluk of Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali and is one of the 30 temples in Central Travancore area where the ancient Dravidian ritualistic art form of Padayani is performed annually.

Manimala River, or Manimalayar, is a 92 km long river which flows through South Kerala. The river used to be wrongly considered as a tributary of Pamba River before satellite maps became popular, but this was proved incorrect.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (92 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (77 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thiruvanvandoor Mahavishnu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mazhukeer, Karingattukavu Devi Temple, Adichikkavu Sree Durga Devi Kshetram, Mundankavu, Kadavil Malika.
